title: Deterministic Tensor Governance via Interface-Led Architecture: A Contract-First Architecture for Governing LLM and Quantum Tensor Providers
description: This position paper proposes Deterministic Tensor Governance, a contract-first governance architecture for non-deterministic tensor providers, including Large Language Models (LLMs) and Quantum Processing Units (QPUs). The paper does not claim physical equivalence between LLM sampling and QPU measurement. Instead, it treats them as architecturally analogous providers at the system boundary and defines a TensorKernel governance layer based on interfaces, contracts, invariants, fail-closed behavior, policy decision points, and replay logs. The English manuscript is the canonical version; the Japanese manuscript is included as a companion translation.
